content: Hi, my internet has been acting up all morning.
Hello! Thanks for calling TechLine support, my name is Alex. I'm sorry to hear about the trouble with your internet. Let me help you get that sorted out.
Can you describe what's happening? Is the connection dropping completely, or is it just slow?
It keeps disconnecting every few minutes. The Wi-Fi light on the router blinks red and then goes back to normal.
Got it — intermittent disconnections with a red blinking light. Have you tried restarting the router already?
Yeah, I restarted it twice this morning. It comes back for a bit and then drops again.
Okay, let's try a full power cycle. Unplug the router from the wall, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in. Let me know once it's fully booted up.
Alright, give me a moment... Okay, it's back on. All lights are green now.
Great, the lights look good. Can you try loading a webpage or running a speed test to see if the connection holds?
It loaded fine but... wait, it just dropped again. The red light is back.
Okay, so the power cycle didn't hold. That red blinking pattern usually points to a firmware or signal issue. Let me check your account for any outages in your area.
Sure, go ahead.

I don't see any outages in your area. But I can see your router's firmware is two versions behind. That could be causing instability. I'm going to push a firmware update remotely — it should take about two minutes. Your internet will drop briefly during the update.
Okay, do it.
The firmware update is complete. Your router should be rebooting now. Can you check if the lights are all green?
Yeah, all green. Let me try browsing... It's working. I'll wait a few minutes to see if it drops again.
Sounds good, take your time. I'll stay on the line.
It's been about three minutes and it's still connected. But now I'm noticing something else — the speed is really slow. Pages take forever to load.
Okay, so the disconnections may be fixed but now we have a speed issue. Can you run a speed test? You can go to speedtest.net and tell me the download and upload numbers.
It says 8 Mbps download and 2 Mbps upload. I'm supposed to be getting 200 Mbps.
That's way below your plan. Let me check the signal levels on your modem from our end.
Sure.
I can see your signal-to-noise ratio is very low — that usually means there's interference or a degraded line. Let me try resetting the modem's connection profile from here.
Go for it.
Done. Your modem is reconnecting now. Give it about a minute and then run the speed test again.
Okay... it's showing 12 Mbps now. Better but still nowhere near 200.
Yeah, 12 Mbps is still way off. Given that the firmware update and modem reset didn't fully resolve this, I suspect there may be a physical line issue or equipment problem that I can't fix remotely.
So what happens now?

I'm going to escalate this to our senior network team. They'll be able to run deeper diagnostics and dispatch a technician if needed. I've created ticket number TL-20260416-0892 for you. You should receive a callback within 4 hours.
Alright, that works. Will they have all the info from this call?
Yes, I've attached all the details — the disconnection issue, the firmware update we did, the modem reset, and the speed test results. They'll have the full picture.
Okay. Thanks for trying at least.

Of course! So to summarize: we fixed the intermittent disconnections with a firmware update, but the slow speed issue needs a deeper look. Your ticket is TL-20260416-0892 and the senior team will call you back within 4 hours. Is there anything else I can help with?
No, that's everything. Thanks Alex.
You're welcome! Thanks for calling TechLine, and I hope we get your speed sorted out quickly. Have a great day!
